campaigns.title: Help 72-year-old Vanitha provide 100+ abandoned mentally ill women with shelter and care
campaigns.description: “The major problem in society today is the stigma against mentally ill or mentally disabled people. Families feed and care for men who are mentally ill and keep them at home. But no one wants to help women with mental illnesses,” says N Vanitha Rengaraj, chairperson of Sharanalayam.Sharanalaym’s ‘Jothi’ is a sanctuary for over 100 women facing mental challenges. Women with mental illness facing various challenges on the streets are brought to Sharanalayam with the help of the authorities and cared for. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Donations to this fundraiser are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).The women at Sharanalaym Jothi are provided with necessities like food, clothing and medical care. They also get mental health services, access to psychiatrists and counseling, empowering individuals on their path to recovery.Once lost and vulnerable on unfamiliar streets, the women at Sharanalaym Jothi find solace.By supporting mentally disabled women at Sharanalaym Jothi, you contribute to creating a better world. We can ensure these women receive the care and support they deserve.Over the years, Vanitha has tried her best to run Sharanalayam from her own resources and that of her well-wishers, but the last few years have been harsh. “I normally don’t extend my hand for people to donate. Many have come on their own. But life has become a bit difficult now. I need your support to take care of the women here and also bring more women here in need of help,” says Vanitha. How Sharanalayam came into beingThe NGO started its operations in 2001 from rented premises in Pollachi in the district of Coimbatore. It started with taking care of children in difficult conditions. It slowly started taking care of mentally challenged women. Sharanalayam has come a long way since that time, with hundreds of children and women being taken care of by the NGO in the last two decades. Many women were rehabilitated by the NGO, thanks to its facilities.Sharanalayam needs urgent support to take care of over 100 women. The organisation also aspires to take care of many more mentally ill women in the coming months and years. Vanitha cannot do it without your support. Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help abandoned mentally ill women get care and shelter. Vanitha, 72, the founder of Sharanalayam cannot do it alone.Donate now.

messages.en:
At 72, Vanitha has dedicated her life to caring for 100+ abandoned mentally ill women who have nowhere else to go. These vulnerable women depend on her shelter for basic dignity and care.

Only 2 days left to raise ₹1.9 crore more (₹1.1 crore raised of ₹3 crore goal). Even ₹100 helps.

Please donate OR share with 5 people

campaigns.title: Help Gopalan of Akshaya Trust feed and care for rescued abandoned elderly parents
campaigns.description: “Parvathi Amma (name changed) was brought to Akshaya Trust by her son. He brought her here saying she was being taken to the hospital for treatment and he would be back in a while. It's been 8 years since that day. The phone number he gave to us was made up, even his address was false. Imagine the pain of the mother who was abandoned in such a heartless manner. She is not the only one. We have over 260 elderly parents here and most of them have faced abandonment, neglect or even abuse”, says G Gopalan, the founder of Akshaya Trust, the NGO taking care of abandoned seniors who have no one else in the world.Without Gopalan’s Akshaya Trust, countless elderly would still be on the streets or simply deprived of the love and care they deserve. The NGO takes care of every single need of its seniors from Food to medical care and housing. Your donation can enable Akshaya Trust to reach out to many more elderly with food and support. Contribute generously. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).G Gopalan, a well-to-do businessman preferred to serve the elderly than living a peaceful retired life. He says that he was too shocked to see the state of the elderly, treated shabbily by their own children.A lifeline for hundreds of neglected elderlyToday, Akshaya Trust offers a safe haven to over 260 elderly parents, shielding them from the harsh realities of street life. This organization attends to all their needs, providing comprehensive medical care and ensuring they recover from past traumas. The trust also organises joyful celebrations during festivals and special occasions, bringing happiness into their lives.To date, Akshaya Trust has served over 1,000 elderly parents most of whom were from extremely weak financial backgrounds and had no way to fend for themselves. Akshaya Trust: A silver lining for elderlyAkshaya Trust’s efforts are a lifeline for these seniors, but they rely heavily on public support to continue. You can be the hero these elderly parents deserve. Donate now to help care for them.Your contribution can transform the lives of many homeless seniors. Support Akshaya Trust’s mission today.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help elderly parents abandoned by their children get the love and care they deserve.

messages.en:
Gopalan has dedicated his life to rescuing abandoned elderly parents and giving them love through Akshaya Trust. These forgotten souls deserve dignity in their final years.

Only 3 days left to raise ₹1.8 crore more (₹1.2 crore raised of ₹3 crore goal). Even ₹100 helps save lives.

campaigns.title: 400 elderly parents abandoned by their families need your urgent support. Donate Now
campaigns.description: Vijaya was around 70 years old and a street in Avadi, Chennai was her home. Abandoned by family, she had no roof to call home and no means of survival. The police had warned her to move on. Residents had complained. Nobody wanted to touch what they saw as somebody else's problem. Then someone called Anbagam. The rescue team from the NGO arrived. There was a complication: Vijaya refused to leave without her puppies. The team found homes for each one. Then they brought Vijaya in. Today she is healthy, comfortable, and happy. This is what Anbagam does. It meets people exactly where they are, asks nothing of them except to come, and gives them something most of us take entirely for granted: a safe place to be.But without your support this safe haven for the elderly might not even exist. Be the pillar of support this old age home needs. Donate generously!Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A life-long missionSince 1999, Mohammed Rafi, a small businessman from Parry's in Chennai, has been doing this work. He started by housing people in his own home. When that filled up, he built more. Today, Anbagam runs three homes across Chennai and Thiruvallur, caring for 400 residents: mentally ill, elderly, abandoned, forgotten.Running this for 400 people costs money. Food alone costs roughly Rs 4 lakh every month. Medicines and psychiatric care cost approximately Rs 1 lakh a month. Add staff, a doctor, cleaning, bedding, and clothing, and the annual operating cost across all three homes comes to around Rs 77 lakh.Your contribution means a warm meal, a comfortable bed and necessary medical treatment for an abandoned elderly grandparent. Support Now!Impact beyond numbersOver 7,000 people have walked through Anbagam's doors. Around 3,000 have been reunited with families across India. The rest stay, because Anbagam is the family they have now.Mohammed Rafi said it simply: “Share your happiness with the people who are here. Help them!”Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Anbagam, in its mission to give abandoned elderly parents the care they deserve.

messages.en:
400 elderly parents have been abandoned by their own children and desperately need our help for basic care and medical support. These senior citizens, who once cared for their families, now have nowhere to turn.

Only 3 days left to raise ₹48.4 lakh more - we've raised just ₹1.6 lakh so far.

Even ₹100 helps save lives. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: Help visually impaired ‘Singh Sir’ provide food, shelter and education to blind girls
campaigns.description: In many families, blind girls are often seen as a burden—a harsh reality that leaves many of them abandoned, neglected, and deprived of opportunities. Families often feel, "If we spend money on them, it would be useless", shares Ramesh Singh a blind teacher committed to providing care and education the blind girls deserve. Sadly, many blind girls are forced to grow up in isolation and denied the education and care they deserve. But amidst this darkness, there is a glimmer of hope. “Singh Sir” as he is fondly called, is on a mission but he cannot do it alone. Support this blind teacher in transforming lives. Donate generouslyDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).How it beganIn 2005, Ramesh Prasad Singh, a blind teacher who has personally experienced the struggles faced by blind children, founded the Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad. His mission? To rescue blind girls from neglect and give them a chance to thrive."People think this girl is a lifelong burden for us," Ramesh says. "But as soon as the girl gets a job, the same family starts hounding her."Over the years, the nonprofit has provided food, shelter, and admission fees for college to blind girls, ensuring they are not trapped within four walls but instead empowered to pursue their dreams.At the heart of Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad is a home—a safe haven for blind girls where they receive education, care, and the tools to build a better future. These girls are not just surviving; they are thriving. They are becoming independent, proving that blindness is not a limitation but a stepping stone to resilience and strength.But Ramesh cannot continue this mission alone.He appeals to you to join hands and support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad.Why your contribution countsNutritious meals for blind girlsSafe and secure shelterAdmission fees and educational support"We appeal to each person to support our mission and make a difference," Ramesh urges.Together, we can ensure these girls are no longer seen as burdens but as empowered individuals ready to lead fulfilling lives.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad in its mission to provide blind girls the shelter, care and education they deserve.

messages.en:
Singh Sir is visually impaired but has dedicated his life to providing food, shelter and education to blind girls who have nowhere else to go.

His shelter needs urgent support - only 5 days left to raise ₹11.8 lakh more (₹38.2L raised of ₹50L goal).

Even ₹100 helps these girls continue their education.

Please donate OR share with 5 people 🙏

campaigns.title: Help Vinay bring children facing abuse in red-light areas to safety and protect them
campaigns.description: “I saw a girl, barely 7 or 8, being pushed into flesh trade. Her cries for help still echo in my ears. There were men as old as 60 r*ping the innocent child. That was the day I knew my mission in life was to get these children out of the hell-like conditions in red-light areas. They are nothing short of hell and no child deserves to grow up in this environment” says Vinay Vasta, founder of Social Activities Integration, an organisation committed to freeing children trapped in red-light areas.Vinay and his NGO have been able to provide education, medical care and nutrition to over 2,500 children. But his dream to provide safe shelter to children remains unrealized. “We do our best to keep these children safe during the day but sadly they are forced to go back to the red light areas at night as we don’t have a home for them. Your donation can help us build a safe home for children of sex workers and prevent them from being pulled into prostitution. Donate to make this dream a realityDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Life in red light areas“S*x workers use their homes which are barely 6 feet by 4 feet for their business. They invite customers in the presence of their children. Their children not only witness abuse but experience it too. Just imagine a child being told that all she can ever become in life is a pr*stitute”, Vinay shares as he explains the extent of abuse children face in red light areas.This is a life that thousands of children are forced to live behind closed doors where they suffer abuse and exploitation morning, noon and night.A ray of hopeVinay has been relentlessly trying to give children the care and education they deserve. He has provided quality English education to thousands of children, enabling them to secure a livelihood out of red-light areas. Several children taught by Vinay are today leading successful lives as professionals and Government employees. However the sad reality is that Vinay and his NGO, in the absence of a permanent shelter home, are unable to provide round the clock safety to these children, which is essential for their well-being.Your donations can enable SAI to build a shelter home for the children of sex workers and usher in a new tomorrow for them, away from the horrors of red-light areas.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to provide safe shelter for children of sex workers and save them from abuse and second generation pr*stitution.NGO's 80G number: AACTS0408EF20216NGO's 12A number: AACTS0408EE20028

messages.en:
A father's worst nightmare - Subramanian Ganesan suffered a brain stroke and his family is desperately trying to save him. His son is watching his father fight for his life while medical bills pile up.

They need ₹20.4 lakh more in just 17 days. Even ₹100 helps bring him closer to recovery.

Please donate OR share with 5 people - every action saves a life.

messages.gu:
એક પિતાનું સૌથી ભયાનક સ્વપ્ન - સુબ્રમણ્યન ગણેશનને બ્રેઈન સ્ટ્રોક થયો છે અને તેમનો પરિવાર તેમને બચાવવા માટે સંઘર્ષ કરી રહ્યો છે. તેમનો પુત્ર પોતાના પિતાને જીવન માટે લડતા જોઈ રહ્યો છે.

માત્ર 17 દિવસમાં ₹20.4 લાખની જરૂર છે. ₹100 પણ તેમની સ્વસ્થતામાં મદદ કરશે.

કૃપા કરીને દાન કરો અથવા 5 લોકો સાથે શેર કરો - દરેક મદદ એક જીવન બચાવે છે.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1
messages.pa:
ਇੱਕ ਪਿਤਾ ਦਾ ਸਭ ਤੋਂ ਭਿਆਨਕ ਸੁਪਨਾ - ਸੁਬ੍ਰਮਣਿਅਨ ਗਣੇਸ਼ਨ ਨੂੰ ਬ੍ਰੇਨ ਸਟ੍ਰੋਕ ਹੋਇਆ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਉਸਦਾ ਪਰਿਵਾਰ ਉਸਨੂੰ ਬਚਾਉਣ ਲਈ ਸੰਘਰਸ਼ ਕਰ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ। ਉਸਦਾ ਪੁੱਤਰ ਆਪਣੇ ਪਿਤਾ ਨੂੰ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਦੀ ਲੜਾਈ ਲੜਦੇ ਦੇਖ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ।

ਸਿਰਫ 17 ਦਿਨਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ₹20.4 ਲੱਖ ਚਾਹੀਦੇ ਹਨ। ₹100 ਵੀ ਉਸਦੀ ਰਿਕਵਰੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਦਦ ਕਰੇਗਾ।

ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਦਾਨ ਕਰੋ ਜਾਂ 5 ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਸਾਂਝਾ ਕਰੋ - ਹਰ ਮਦਦ ਇੱਕ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਬਚਾਉਂਦੀ ਹੈ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1
messages.ml:
ഒരു അച്ഛന്റെ ഏറ്റവും ഭയാനകമായ സ്വപ്നം - സുബ്രഹ്മണ്യൻ ഗണേശന് ബ്രെയിൻ സ്ട്രോക്ക് വന്നിട്ടുണ്ട്, അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ കുടുംബം അദ്ദേഹത്തെ രക്ഷിക്കാൻ പരിശ്രമിക്കുന്നു. അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ മകൻ തന്റെ അച്ഛൻ ജീവനുവേണ്ടി പോരാടുന്നത് കാണുകയാണ്।

വെറും 17 ദിവസത്തിനുള്ളിൽ ₹20.4 ലക്ഷം വേണം. ₹100 പോലും അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ വേഗത്തിലുള്ള സുഖപ്രാപ്തിയെ സഹായിക്കും।

ദയവായി സംഭാവന നൽകുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 5 പേരുമായി പങ്കിടുക - ഓരോ സഹായവും ഒരു ജീവൻ രക്ഷിക്കുന്നു।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1

campaigns.title: Help Our Teen Daughter Fight Brain Tumor
campaigns.description: My name is Kabita, I am a house maid, and I am raising funds for my 15-year-old daughter, who lives with us in Hyderabad...

messages.en: Our friend Kabita's teenage daughter is fighting a brain tumor. This brave girl needs our help for her treatment. Only 21 days left to raise ₹5.9 lakh - they've raised just ₹6K so far. Even ₹100 helps save her life. Please donate OR share with 5 people. https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.hi: हमारी मित्र कबिता की किशोर बेटी ब्रेन ट्यूमर से लड़ रही है। इस बहादुर बच्ची को इलाज के लिए हमारी मदद चाहिए। सिर्फ 21 दिन बचे हैं ₹5.9 लाख जुटाने के लिए - अभी तक केवल ₹6 हजार मिले हैं। ₹100 भी उसकी जान बचा सकते हैं। कृपया दान करें या 5 लोगों के साथ शेयर करें। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.kn: ನಮ್ಮ ಸ್ನೇಹಿತ ಕಬಿತಾ ಅವರ ಹದಿಹರೆಯದ ಮಗಳು ಮೆದುಳಿನ ಗೆಡ್ಡೆಯೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹೋರಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾಳೆ. ಈ ಧೈರ್ಯವಂತ ಹುಡುಗಿಗೆ ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆಗಾಗಿ ನಮ್ಮ ಸಹಾಯ ಬೇಕು. ₹5.9 ಲಕ್ಷ ಸಂಗ್ರಹಿಸಲು ಕೇವಲ 21 ದಿನಗಳು ಬಾಕಿ - ಇಲ್ಲಿಯವರೆಗೆ ಕೇವಲ ₹6 ಸಾವಿರ ಸಿಕ್ಕಿದೆ. ₹100 ಕೂಡ ಅವಳ ಜೀವ ಉಳಿಸಲು ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡುತ್ತದೆ. ದಯವಿಟ್ಟು ದಾನ ಮಾಡಿ ಅಥವಾ 5 ಜನರೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹಂಚಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.ta: நமது நண்பர் கபிதாவின் இளம்வயது மகள் மூளைக் கட்டியுடன் போராடிக் கொண்டிருக்கிறாள். இந்த தைரியமான பெண்ணுக்கு சிகிச்சைக்காக நமது உதவி தேவை. ₹5.9 லட்சம் சேகரிக்க வெறும் 21 நாட்கள் மட்டுமே உள்ளன - இதுவரை வெறும் ₹6 ஆயிரம் மட்டுமே கிடைத்துள்ளது. ₹100 கூட அவளது உயிரைக் காப்பாற்ற உதவும். தயவுசெய்து நன்கொடை அளியுங்கள் அல்லது 5 பேரிடம் பகிருங்கள். https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.te: మా స్నేహితురాలు కబితా కుమార్తె యువతి మెదడు కణితితో పోరాడుతోంది. ఈ ధైర్యవంతమైన అమ్మాయికి చికిత్స కోసం మన సహాయం కావాలి. ₹5.9 లక్షలు సేకరించడానికి కేవలం 21 రోజులు మిగిలి ఉన్నాయి - ఇప్పటివరకు కేవలం ₹6 వేలు మాత్రమే వచ్చాయి. ₹100 కూడా ఆమె ప్రాణాన్ని కాపాడటానికి సహాయపడుతుంది. దయచేసి దానం చేయండి లేదా 5 మందితో పంచుకోండి। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.mr: आमची मैत्रिण कबिताची किशोरवयीन मुलगी ब्रेन ट्यूमरशी लढत आहे. या धाडसी मुलीला उपचारासाठी आमची मदत हवी आहे. ₹5.9 लाख गोळा करण्यासाठी फक्त 21 दिवस उरले आहेत - आत्तापर्यंत फक्त ₹6 हजार मिळाले आहेत. ₹100 सुद्धा तिचे जीवन वाचवू शकते. कृपया दान करा किंवा 5 लोकांसोबत शेअर करा। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.bn: আমাদের বন্ধু কবিতার কিশোরী মেয়ে ব্রেন টিউমারের সাথে লড়াই করছে। এই সাহসী মেয়েটির চিকিৎসার জন্য আমাদের সাহায্য প্রয়োজন। ₹5.9 লক্ষ সংগ্রহ করার জন্য মাত্র 21 দিন বাকি - এখন পর্যন্ত মাত্র ₹6 হাজার পেয়েছে। এমনকি ₹100 ও তার জীবন বাঁচাতে সাহায্য করবে। অনুগ্রহ করে দান করুন বা 5 জনের সাথে শেয়ার করুন। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.gu: અમારી મિત્ર કબિતાની કિશોરવયની દીકરી મગજના ગાંઠ સામે લડી રહી છે. આ બહાદુર છોકરીને સારવાર માટે અમારી મદદની જરૂર છે. ₹5.9 લાખ એકત્રિત કરવા માટે માત્ર 21 દિવસ બાકી છે - અત્યાર સુધી માત્ર ₹6 હજાર મળ્યા છે. ₹100 પણ તેનો જીવ બચાવવામાં મદદ કરશે. કૃપા કરીને દાન આપો અથવા 5 લોકો સાથે શેર કરો। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.pa: ਸਾਡੀ ਸਹੇਲੀ ਕਬਿਤਾ ਦੀ ਨੌਜਵਾਨ ਧੀ ਦਿਮਾਗੀ ਗਿਲਟੀ ਨਾਲ ਲੜ ਰਹੀ ਹੈ। ਇਸ ਬਹਾਦਰ ਕੁੜੀ ਨੂੰ ਇਲਾਜ ਲਈ ਸਾਡੀ ਮਦਦ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। ₹5.9 ਲੱਖ ਇਕੱਠੇ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ ਸਿਰਫ 21 ਦਿਨ ਬਾਕੀ ਹਨ - ਹੁਣ ਤੱਕ ਸਿਰਫ ₹6 ਹਜਾਰ ਮਿਲੇ ਹਨ। ਇੱਥੋਂ ਤੱਕ ਕਿ ₹100 ਵੀ ਉਸਦੀ ਜਿੰਦਗੀ ਬਚਾਉਣ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਦਦ ਕਰਦੇ ਹਨ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਦਾਨ ਦਿਓ ਜਾਂ 5 ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਸ਼ੇਅਰ ਕਰੋ। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.ml: ഞങ്ങളുടെ സുഹൃത്തായ കബിതയുടെ കൗമാരക്കാരിയായ മകൾ ബ്രെയിൻ ട്യൂമറുമായി പോരാടുകയാണ്. ഈ ധീരയായ പെൺകുട്ടിക്ക് ചികിത്സയ്ക്കായി നമ്മുടെ സഹായം ആവശ്യമുണ്ട്. ₹5.9 ലക്ഷം സമാഹരിക്കാൻ വെറും 21 ദിവസങ്ങൾ മാത്രം ബാക്കി - ഇതുവരെ വെറും ₹6 ആയിരം മാത്രമേ കിട്ടിയുള്ളൂ. ₹100 പോലും അവളുടെ ജീവൻ രക്ഷിക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കും. ദയവായി സംഭാവന നൽകുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 5 പേരുമായി പങ്കുവെക്കുക। https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j

campaigns.title: Help my sister recover from multiple critical fractures
campaigns.description: I am raising funds for my sister, Ms. Imagineia Ryngkhlem, who is 28 years old and currently battling the aftermath of s...

messages.en:
My sister is fighting for her life after suffering multiple critical fractures. She's always been the strong one in our family, but now she needs us.

We have only 23 days left and still need ₹19.9 lakh for her treatment. Even ₹100 helps save her.

Please donate OR share with 5 people - both make a difference.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-ms-imagineia-ryngkhlem
messages.hi:
मेरी बहन कई गंभीर फ्रैक्चर के बाद जिंदगी की लड़ाई लड़ रही है। वह हमेशा से परिवार की मजबूत कड़ी रही है, लेकिन अब उसे हमारी जरूरत है।

सिर्फ 23 दिन बचे हैं और अभी भी ₹19.9 लाख की जरूरत है। ₹100 भी उसकी जान बचा सकते हैं।

कृपया दान करें या 5 लोगों के साथ शेयर करें - दोनों से मदद मिलेगी।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-ms-imagineia-ryngkhlem
messages.kn:
ನನ್ನ ಅಕ್ಕ ಅನೇಕ ಗಂಭೀರ ಮೂಳೆ ಮುರಿತಗಳ ನಂತರ ಜೀವನಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಹೋರಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾಳೆ. ಅವಳು ಯಾವಾಗಲೂ ನಮ್ಮ ಕುಟುಂಬದ ಬಲಿಷ್ಠ ಆಧಾರ, ಆದರೆ ಈಗ ಅವಳಿಗೆ ನಮ್ಮ ಅಗತ್ಯವಿದೆ।

ಕೇವಲ 23 ದಿನಗಳು ಬಾಕಿ ಮತ್ತು ಇನ್ನೂ ₹19.9 ಲಕ್ಷದ ಅಗತ್ಯವಿದೆ. ₹100 ಸಹ ಅವಳ ಜೀವ ಉಳಿಸಬಹುದು।

ದಯವಿಟ್ಟು ದಾನ ಮಾಡಿ ಅಥವಾ 5 ಜನರೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹಂಚಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ - ಎರಡೂ ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡುತ್ತದೆ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-ms-imagineia-ryngkhlem
messages.ta:
எனது அக்கா பல கடுமையான எலும்பு முறிவுகளுக்கு பிறகு உயிருக்காக போராடிக்கொண்டிருக்கிறாள். அவள் எங்கள் குடும்பத்தின் வலிமையான தூண், ஆனால் இப்போது அவளுக்கு நமது உதவி தேவை।

வெறும் 23 நாட்கள் மட்டுமே உள்ளது, இன்னும் ₹19.9 லட்சம் தேவை. ₹100 கூட அவளது உயிரைக் காப்பாற்றலாம்.

தயவுசெய்து நன்கொடை அளிக்கவும் அல்லது 5 பேருடன் பகிர்ந்து கொள்ளவும் - இரண்டும் உதவும்.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-ms-imagineia-ryngkhlem
messages.te:
నా అక్కడు అనేక తీవ్రమైన ఎముక పగుళ్ల తర్వాత ప్రాణాల కోసం పోరాడుతోంది. ఆమె ఎల్లప్పుడూ మా కుటుంబానికి బలమైన మద్దతు, కానీ ఇప్పుడు ఆమెకు మన సహాయం కావాలి।

కేవలం 23 రోజులు మాత్రమే మిగిలి ఉన్నాయి మరియు ఇంకా ₹19.9 లక్షలు అవసరం. ₹100 కూడా ఆమె ప్రాణాలను కాపాడగలవు।

దయచేసి విరాళం ఇవ్వండి లేదా 5 మందితో పంచుకోండి - రెండూ సహాయపడతాయి.

messages.mr:

campaigns.title: Support Quality Education For Children
campaigns.description: Think back to your school days: what did you discover about yourself?A time you felt proud of yourself.A story that stayed with you.A moment that sparked your curiosity.A subject or sport that you loved. These discoveries don’t happen by accident. They happen in schools that make space for curiosity, participation, and exploration.Every child deserves an education that makes room for this discovery.At Adhyayan Foundation, we work with government schools to help create joyful learning environments where children have a voice and can discover what they’re good at.To continue and expand this work in underserved regions of the country, we are raising Rs 50 lakhs. Donate to support discoveries!What your support makes possibleOver the past nine years, we’ve partnered with education systems across Arunachal Pradesh, Delhi, Goa, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Nagaland, and Tripura, reaching more than 10,000 schools and 6,000 village libraries.We support school leaders and teachers to reflect on their strengths, challenges, and areas of growth, and support them in taking practical steps toward school improvement according to their unique contexts. On the way, we co-create avenues for support and problem-solving for teachers and school heads.This leads to improvements in how a child experiences learning in school. Classrooms become more interactive, reading spaces come alive, student attendance improves, the school environment becomes more child-friendly, and the school’s local community becomes more involved.An engaging science lesson enabled by school-to-school resource sharing in Tripura.Students discovering a love for football in our Field Connect program in GoaA moment of discovery in a government school we work with in Arunachal Pradesh.A reading corner in a government school in Kalyan: students are fascinated by the books and regularly browse through them.Students discover career options through exposure visits through our Career Compass program in GoaThis year, we’re celebrating the everyday discoveries that happen in good schools: discoveries of words, ideas, stories, confidence, curiosity, and voice.Your support helps create the conditions that make these discoveries possible, from better teaching practices to stronger school leadership and more engaging learning spaces.The AskWe are raising Rs. 50 lakh by 15th June 2026 to continue and expand our work in underserved regions.Your donation supports our team, training, innovations & pilots, and the essential systems behind our work as part of our working capital reserve, ensuring that our programs remain consistent, responsive, and resilient. This means:Schools receive continuous supportTeachers can keep improving their practiceCommunities can rely on us as long-term partners, not just when funding cycles align.Your contribution would enable consistency and reliability on the ground, helping programs bring lasting impact for the children and those supporting them in the ecosystem.Every child deserves a school where they can explore, participate, and discover.Donate now to support quality education in government schools in India!Learn more about our work on our website and our most recent annual report.

campaigns.title: Help provide safe access to play for 250 children in Raigarh
campaigns.description: When Durga first joined KhelMel's community sports programme in Raigarh, she came once, maybe twice a month. Sport had never felt like it was meant for her. "I didn't think anyone would notice if I was there or not." - DurgaTwo years later, Durga is the captain of her team.She coordinates warmups, plans games, and checks in with teammates. She mediates disagreements on the ground. She plans strategies before matches, and all this is because of one simple step- access to sports. And the program ensured that she needs to show up consistently because the ground, her team and coaches would be there next week too.The problem we are solvingChildren in rural communities face barriers that limit their access to safe, inclusive, and structured opportunities for play and sports.These barriers are not just physical, no ground, no equipment. They are social: gender norms that keep girls off the field, poverty that sends children to work instead of play, and bullying that makes showing up feel unsafe. A child without structured sport misses not just a game, but the confidence, friendships, and emotional resilience that grow through it.KhelMel exists to hold that window open - KhelMel uses community sport to help children from marginalised backgrounds in Raigarh build emotional strength, confidence, and belonging. Our Ask:250 children will be supported through our programme this year in Raigarh, Chhattisgarh. Cost to support one child for a full year- INR 8,500, which includes:Sports infrastructure Trained coach supportNutritional supportWhy Support us:Appreciated by the District Collector for promoting ‘Sports for Mental Well-being’Acknowledged for our work by UNICEF for sport & gender equitySelected as Education Partner by Wipro FoundationThe girls' team came runner up at the district level tournament held inWant to understand more about our work, our impact, or how your donation is used? We would love to hear from you.Reach us at kushal@khelmel.in / +91 79924 70486

campaigns.title: Fuel their Future- Nutritious Meals for Young Minds
campaigns.description: For many children from vulnerable communities, the mid-day meal they receive at school is often the most nutritious meal of their day. Without proper nutrition, children struggle to concentrate in class, participate confidently, and learn to their full potential.“Fuel Their Future” is a fundraising campaign in support of Concern India Foundation to help provide nutritious meals for underprivileged school children. Through this initiative, we hope to support healthy meals that nourish young minds, improve well-being, and help children stay focused in school.A simple meal can do more than satisfy hunger — it can give a child the energy to learn, grow, dream, and build a brighter future.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.
